get.summary: Primary purpose: a lightweight root/health endpoint that confirms the Speech-to-Text + Grammar Corre
get.description: Primary purpose: a lightweight root/health endpoint that confirms the Speech-to-Text + Grammar Correction API is running and reachable by returning a simple status message. Business context: call this endpoint for uptime checks, readiness/liveness probes, quick integration tests, or to verify the service before invoking heavier endpoints. Error scenarios: the implementation returns a static success payload so application-level errors are unlikely; however, infrastructure or runtime failures (e.g., framework exceptions, deployment issues, or network problems) could produce 5xx responses — 4xx client errors are not expected for this endpoint. Performance and security: the endpoint is inexpensive and cacheable and does not perform pagination; it can be polled frequently for health checks but should be protected by rate limiting and any organization-level authentication measures in production — no authentication/authorization is evident in the code.

example.message: Speech-to-Text + Grammar Correction API is running 🚀

post.summary: Primary purpose: accepts an uploaded audio file, runs a transcription and post-processing pipeline,
post.description: Primary purpose: accepts an uploaded audio file, runs a transcription and post-processing pipeline, and returns corrected transcription text plus a generated .docx file. Business context: use this endpoint to convert meeting recordings, interviews, or other audio into cleaned, human-readable text and a downloadable document; language, engine, and llm_engine form fields allow selecting processing parameters. Error scenarios: the endpoint returns a 400 when transcription fails (e.g., empty or unpr

get.summary: Primary Purpose: Returns a generated DOCX file for download when given a filename path parameter; on
get.description: Primary Purpose: Returns a generated DOCX file for download when given a filename path parameter; on success it streams the file with the Word document media type. Business Context: Use this endpoint when a client needs to retrieve a previously generated DOCX (for example, a report or export) by specifying its filename; it is intended for direct file download rather than JSON data. Error Scenarios: If the file does not exist the endpoint returns a 404 with a JSON body containing an "error" message; other failures (filesystem or IO errors) would surface as server errors (500) though not explicitly handled in the code. Performance & Security Notes: The implementation streams the file (no pagination) and may serve large payloads — consider rate limiting, response caching, or streaming limits in production; no authentication/authorization is evident in the code, so secure access controls should be added in real deployments. Inferred fields: the request path parameter "filename" and the 404 JSON response field "error" are inferred from the function code.
